page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m4.large is a burstable virtual machine instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, 8.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core, and no local storage or bundled GPUs. The CPU features a 33 MB L3 cache and supports AVX and AVX512F vector extensions.

Benchmark results indicate that this instance is positioned in the lower performance tiers. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is rated as weak at 1057.79, while multi-core performance falls into the poor category at 2090.03. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with large-block read-only bandwidth measured at 16.3 GB/sec. In application-level benchmarks, the instance achieved 2.54 million operations per second in Redis SET operations and 1.48 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

Due to its burstable CPU allocation model and baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps, this instance is designed for budget-conscious scenarios where continuous high-performance compute is not required. The resource density provides a cost-effective entry point for workloads with low baseline CPU utilization that experience occasional spikes. However, the lack of local storage and low benchmark scores across computational, memory, and database operations represent significant tradeoffs for resource-intensive applications.

This instance is best suited for light, non-critical workloads. Appropriate use cases include low-traffic web serving, development and testing environments, small code repositories, and basic microservices that do not require sustained high CPU throughput or high-speed local storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m4.large is a burstable, budget-friendly virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 2 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8163 processor operating at 2.5 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark data places this server in the lower performance tiers, with weak single-core and poor multi-core CPU metrics, alongside limited memory bandwidth. Given its burstable CPU allocation and cost-effective resource profile, the ecs.t5-c1m4.large is designed for low-intensity workloads. It is best suited for lightweight applications such as development environments, low-traffic web servers, and basic background tasks that require minimal sustained compute resources.
